reviews.title_a: Compares alternative thresholds using performance evidence
reviews.title_b: Justifies model or threshold choice with evidence
reviews.explanation: OB_STR_001 requires comparative performance evidence across alternatives; OB_STR_003 requires post-choice rationale. A learner may compare without justifying, or justify a choice made without explicit comparison — separate evidentiary signatures.

reviews.title_a: Compares alternative thresholds using performance evidence
reviews.explanation: OB_STR_001 is pairwise comparison; OB_STR_008 is selection of optimum across a structured candidate set (table/conditions). Optimization presupposes comparison but adds global selection criterion — not redundant.

reviews.title_a: Selects threshold or feature using explicit criteria
reviews.title_b: Justifies model or threshold choice with evidence
reviews.explanation: Sequential strategic chain: selection (OB_STR_002) often precedes justification (OB_STR_003). Compositional, not duplicate — retain both for traceability of partial competence.

reviews.title_a: Justifies model or threshold choice with evidence
reviews.title_b: Synthesizes exploration into justified parameter choice
reviews.review_risk: Justify vs synthesize exploration into choice
reviews.explanation: OB_STR_007 subsumes justification as part of synthesis after exploration. Distinct because synthesis requires linking multiple trials to a final choice — higher coordination demand.

reviews.title_a: Iteratively explores parameter combinations affecting performance
reviews.explanation: OB_STR_006 is process evidence (iteration); OB_STR_008 is outcome evidence (best configuration). Digital fluency may inflate 006 without supporting 008.

reviews.title_a: Applies threshold to classify a case
reviews.title_b: Sets split thresholds at root or inner nodes
reviews.review_risk: Apply threshold vs set split thresholds
reviews.explanation: OB_PRO_001 is case classification via threshold; OB_PRO_007 is authorship of split values during construction. Same operator, different task demand — procedural granularity justified.

reviews.title_a: Applies threshold to classify a case
reviews.review_risk: Single threshold apply vs path traversal
reviews.explanation: Single-step threshold application differs from multi-node path traversal. Co-occurrence does not collapse behaviours.

reviews.title_a: Explains purpose and mechanism of decision-tree classification
reviews.review_risk: Explain mechanism vs articulate if-then rule
reviews.explanation: Cross-dimension: conceptual mechanism explanation (CON) vs procedural rule articulation (PRO). CLT-D guard applies — fluent rules without mechanism understanding.

reviews.title_a: States or explains evaluation metric definitions
reviews.title_b: Computes metric values from given data
reviews.review_risk: State metric definition vs compute metric
reviews.explanation: Metric definition recall differs from computation execution — classic declarative/procedural split.

reviews.title_a: Identifies predictive features from observed data patterns
reviews.review_risk: Data-grounded feature ID vs uninformed prior
reviews.explanation: OB_REF_004 is baseline uninformed prior (weak ceiling); OB_STR_004 requires cited data pattern — critical for CLT-A/B separation.

reviews.title_a: Reflects on learning or misconceptions about decision trees
reviews.review_risk: Reflect on learning vs articulate limitations
reviews.explanation: Learning reflection differs from model-limitation discourse; overlap in prose style managed by leakage_guard.
Vocabulary accuracy is prerequisite to distinction; dependency graph encodes requires edge. Not mergeable without losing granularity.

note: Jaccard token overlap is supplementary; this review uses assessment-theoretic discrimination.
